name: Honda Civic (11th Gen)
engine: 1.5L turbo I4 (L15CA) on EX/Touring — 2.0L naturally-aspirated I4 (K20C2) on LX/Sport
transmission: CVT (the Si and Type R are separate 6-speed manuals)
reliability: 9/10 — too new for a long field record, but it carries over the proven 2.0L and 1.5T engines and the CVT from the 10th gen, and the early-1.5T cold-climate oil dilution was sorted generations earlier. Recalls so far have been minor (verify any open campaign by VIN). The interior quality took a clear step up.
cost_to_own: Insurance: Low to average · Maintenance ~$350/yr · Strong resale and still cheap to run
popular_mods: Winter tires on a second set of wheels for cold-climate owners
All-weather liners and tasteful wheel/tire packages
Hondata flash on the 1.5T for those who want a little more — a real, small tuning path shared with the Si
Honest take: the sporty Si and track-ready Type R are the enthusiast versions; the base car is a refined, dependable daily

generations.11th Gen (2022+): Cleaner, more grown-up styling and a genuinely improved interior with real physical controls. Carryover 2.0L (158hp) and 1.5T (180hp) engines with a CVT, in sedan and hatchback bodies; the Si and Type R sit above it.
generations.Sport-trim hierarchy: LX and Sport get the 2.0L; EX and Touring get the 1.5T. The Si (1.5T, 6-speed) and Type R (2.0T, 6-speed) are separate enthusiast models.

common_issues: Too new for a deep reliability record — track open recalls by VIN
Occasional early infotainment/software quirks (improved via updates)
Soft factory paint and easily-curbed alloy wheels — cosmetic
Road noise on coarse surfaces, typical of the class
maintenance_tips: Keep the infotainment and control software updated on early cars
Run 0W-20 full synthetic on the Maintenance Minder
Service the CVT fluid with genuine Honda HCF-2 around 30–40k miles
Cabin filter, brakes, and tire rotations are most of the upkeep
